title: quercus robur subf. tardissima
intro: Quercus robur subf. tardissima, also known as English oak, is a sub-fossil taxon of the Quercus robur species, characterized by its slow growth rate and unique characteristics. Traditionally, the English oak has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its astringent and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been utilized to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Quercus robur subf. tardissima include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tincture, infusion, or decoction, and is often used in herbal remedies, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
